name: Canola Oil
summary: A neutral, low-saturated-fat cooking oil that is high in monounsaturated fat and one of the few common oils to supply the plant omega-3 ALA — with trial evidence for lowering LDL cholesterol.

benefits.mechanism: Canola oil is low in saturated fat and high in monounsaturated fat, a combination that improves blood lipids when it replaces harder fats. A meta-analysis of 13 randomized trials found that canola oil significantly reduced LDL ('bad') cholesterol, total cholesterol, and the LDL-to-HDL ratio — in fact even more than olive oil in the pooled comparison.

citations.title: Comparison of canola oil and olive oil consumption on the serum lipid profile in adults: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Pourrajab et al., Crit Rev Food Sci Nutr 2023)

benefits.claim: A rare cooking-oil source of the plant omega-3 ALA
benefits.mechanism: Unlike most cooking oils, canola supplies alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), the plant-based omega-3 fatty acid the body can partly convert into the same EPA and DHA found in fish. A meta-analysis of randomized trials linked ALA supplementation to an improved cardiovascular risk profile in people with overweight or obesity.

citations.title: Effect of Alpha-Linolenic Acid Supplementation on Cardiovascular Disease Risk Profile in Individuals with Obesity or Overweight: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials (Yin et al., Adv Nutr 2023)

surprisingFacts.text: 'Canola' isn't a plant — it's rapeseed bred in 1970s Canada to strip out bitter, heart-toxic erucic acid. The name is short for 'Canadian oil, low acid.'
